zh.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo是Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo和Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz的女兒，於大約1046年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯。她於1074年7月19日與Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid”在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1106年在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈瓜亞達拉亞拉可爾莫納斯特利 德 三 佩德羅 德 卡德尼亞享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo是Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo和Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz的女儿，于大约1046年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯。她于1074年7月19日与Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid”在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1106年在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈瓜亞達拉亞拉可爾莫納斯特利 德 三 佩德羅 德 卡德尼亞享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Díaz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Rafael, Miguel, Pedro, Roberto.
Spanish (Díaz): patronymic from the medieval personal name Didacus (see Diego ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Diazdeleon
meanings: Spanish: combination of the surname Díaz (see Diaz ) and the name León (see Leon ) with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Diego
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Francisco, Juan, Fernando, Jose, Miguel, Mario, Maximo, Salvador, Aida, Ana, Domingo.

Spanish: from the very common male personal name Diego, which in the Spanish-speaking world is usually taken to be a shortened form of Santiago . However, this may be only a folk etymology. The personal name is found in the Middle Ages in the Latin forms Didacus and Didagus, probably a derivative of Greek didachē ‘doctrine, teaching’. However, in view of the fact that this personal name is unknown outside the Iberian Peninsula, it may actually have a pre-Roman origin.

variants.name: Diez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Fernando, Pedro, Rafael, Roberto, Angel, Eduardo, Mario, Ricardo.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Oviedo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Jose, Miguel, Eduardo, Manuel, Margarita, Rafael, Fernando, Jaime, Luis, Lupe, Mario.
Spanish: habitational name from Oviedo, Castilianized form of Asturian-Leonese Uviéu, the name of the regional capital of Asturias, found in early records in the Latin form Ovetum.

nameMeanings.name: Jimena
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Jiménez (see Jimenez ).

variants.name: Jimenez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Pedro, Mario, Rafael, Raul.
Spanish (Jiménez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Jimeno . Compare Gimenez , Himenez , Jimenes , Jiminez , and Ximenez .

en.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo was born about 1046, in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain as the daughter of Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo and Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz. She married Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” on 19 July 1074, in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1106, in Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer, Guadalajara, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo est née environ 1046, à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne, fille de Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo et Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz. Elle a épousé Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” le 19 juillet 1074, à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1106, à Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer, Guadalajara, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo wurde um 1046 in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien, als Tochter von Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo und Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz geboren. Sie hat am 19. Juli 1074 in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1106 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer, Guadalajara, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo è nata il circa 1046 nel Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna come figlia di Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo e Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz. Lei sposato Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” il 19 luglio 1074, nel Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1106, nel Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer, Guadalajara, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, all'età di 61 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo(은)는 대략 1046년 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스에서 Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo(와)과 Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1074년 7월 19일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스에서 Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid”(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1106년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 구아다라자라 크어 몬아스테리오 데 산 페드로 데 카데n아에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo nasceu aproximadamente 1046, em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha filha de Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo e Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz. Ela casou-se com Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” em 19 de julho de 1074, em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1106, em Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer, Guadalajara, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, com 61 anos.

ru.Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer: монастирио де сан педро де кардеnа, куир, гуадаладжара, кастилла-ла манча, Испания

ru.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo родилась около 1046 г. в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания. Её родителей звали Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo и Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” 19 июля 1074 г. в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1106 г. в местности монастирио де сан педро де кардеnа, куир, гуадаладжара, кастилла-ла манча, Испания в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Jimena Díaz de Oviedo nació aproximadamente 1046, en Burgos, Castilla y León, España como hija de Conde Diego Fernandez de Oviedo y Condesssa Cristina Gundemariz. Se casó con Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar “El Cid” el 19 de julio de 1074, en Burgos, Castilla y León, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1106, en Monasterio de San Pedro de Cardeña, Quer, Guadalajara, Castilla-La Mancha, España, a la edad de 61 años.
